chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Crl. Bail Application No.3103 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). For hearing of bail application 1. For orders on M.A. No.16369/2025 (U/A). 2. For orders on office objection a/w reply at flag ‘’A’’. 3. For orders on M.A. No.16370/2025 (Ex/A). 4. For hearing of bail application..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 10.11.2025 M/s. Syed Maqbool Hussain Shah, Burhan Khan Jagirani and Talha Azim, Advocates for Applicant a/w applicant..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. 1. Urgency granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just exceptions. 4. Through this Crl. Bail Application, the applicants/accused Arshaduddin Ahmed son of Moizuddin Ahmed and Muhammad Azam Basheer son of Muhammad Basheer seek pre-arrest bail in FIR No.843/2025 under Section-504, 506 & 34 PPC r/w Sections 3 & 4 Lawyers Protection Act, 2023, registered at P.S Malir City. Learned counsel for the applicants/ accused inter alia contends that the applicants/accused are innocent and have falsely been implicated in the present case; that the complainant is a lawyer by profession, therefore, the applicants have not approached the trial Court for seeking pre-arrest bail as they have apprehension of their life and they have approached this Court being concurrent jurisdiction. He relied upon the case of Rais Wazir Ahmad v. The State reported in 2004 SCMR 1167 and argued that the applicants can approach this Court directly without exhausting the first remedy under the dictum laid down by the Supreme Court; that the applicants apprehend of their imminent arrest by the hands of the police to disgrace and humiliate them, hence this bail application.

He relied upon the case of Rais Wazir Ahmad v. The State reported in 2004 SCMR 1167 and argued that the applicants can approach this Court directly without exhausting the first remedy under the dictum laid down by the Supreme Court; that the applicants apprehend of their imminent arrest by the hands of the police to disgrace and humiliate them, hence this bail application. Without touching the merits of the case, the applicants are admitted to interim pre-arrest bail subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Fifty Thousand) each and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. The Applicants are directed to join the investigation/ appear before the learned trial Court, as the case may be. Let notice be issued to the Prosecutor General Sindh and complainant. To come up on 28.11.2025 for confirmation of bail or otherwise. JUDGE Ayaz Gul
